---
title: "Leonhard Kestel, MSc"
image: img/profile.png
about:
  template: trestles
  links:
    # - text: LinkedIn
    #   icon: linkedin
    #   url: "https://www.linkedin.com/in/alburycatalina"
    - text: GitHub
      icon: github
      url: "https://github.com/kestelnadomu"
    - text: ORCID
      icon: person-circle
      url: "https://orcid.org/0009-0008-4549-4960"
    # - text: ResearchGate
    #   icon: journal
    #   url: "https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Catalina-Albury"
    # - text: Scholar
    #   icon: journal
    #   url: "https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Catalina-Albury"
    - text: Email
      icon: envelope
      url: "mailto:leo.kestel@lmu.de"
comments: false
---

::: {.column-page}

## About
I am a PhD candidate at the *Social Data Science and AI* Lab, which is part of the Statistics Department of the Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität in Munich. 

With a background in Psychology and Computer Science, I am a native to interdisciplinary research with focus on the application of Machine Learning algorithms in social sciences and the public sector.
  
In my research I try to use Neurosymbolic architectures as an interface to incorporate arbitrary ethical constraints, formalized in logic or as causal graphs, into the machine learning process.

I am a member of the *ADA* initiative and a co-founder of *ondatix Datenwerkstatt GmbH*, which both aim to support the public administration with data scientific expertise and tools. Therefore, we organise workshops and develop applications for public servants.

**Keywords:** Neurosymbolic AI, Algorithmic Fairness, Trustworthy ML


## Affiliations
[<img class="soda">](https://www.stat.lmu.de/soda)
[<img class="mcml">](https://www.mcml.ai)
[<img class="lmu">](https://www.stat.lmu.de)
[<img class="ada">](https://www.ada-oeffentliche-verwaltung.de)
<!-- [<img class="ondatix">](https://www.ondatix.de) -->
